channel.title: Native Cuisine on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Native Cuisine on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: “bush Bread” (bannock) for 1 Scone
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ cup margarine

Instructions

Mix all the above ingredients together to form a cornmeal-like mixture.

Put this mixture in plastic bag for transporting over portages.

When needed, mix with water in a bowl, then dump it in frying pan and cook till brown. (Not too fast.)

item.title: Ann’s Indian Fry Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 heaping Tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp table salt
  • water to mix and oil or lard to fry in

Instructions

METHOD: In a large bowl combine all dry ingredients. Then slowly add cold water as needed. The texture is a thick texture, thicker than pancake batter. Mix well.

Flour the area where you will be rolling out the dough. Drop the whole dough chunk onto flour, and freely sprinkle more flour on this dough. Begin to knead as though making bread, making sure dough is not sticking to your hands. If that happens, just ad a little more flour.

item.title: Asiago Drop Biscuits – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 2 Tbsp sugar (can use Splenda)
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/3 cup margarine
  • 1/3 cup grated Asiago cheese
  • 2 Tbsp chopped herbs (rosemary, thyme, sage,
  • oregano – these can be fresh or dried herbs)

Instructions

Combine dry ingredients. Cut in margarine. Stir in cheese and herbs. Pour buttermilk over flour mixture and stir only enough to form rough batter (don’t over mix)

item.title: Aunt Gert’s Hamburg Soup – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp butter
  • 3 small onions, chopped
  • 3 large carrots, sliced
  • 3 celery stalks, diced
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 – 16 oz can diced tomatoes
  • 3 medium potatoes, diced
  • 1/3 cup macaroni
  • 6 cups water
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Melt butter, add ground beef and cook slightly. Add onions, tomatoes, salt, pepper and water. Bring to a boil. Cover and simmer for 1 hour. Add vegetables and simmer 1 hour longer. Stir in macaroni during the last 15 minutes.

item.title: Baked Indian Pudding – Aboriginal Cooking
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 cups milk
  • 2 level Tablespoon butter
  • 6 eggs
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp ginger
  • 1 cup Indian meal (Cornmeal)
  • 1 cup powdered sugar (Fruit)

Instructions

Scald 4 cups milk. Mix Indian Meal with 1 cup cold milk and stir into hot milk. Add butter. Continue to stir until mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat.

Beat eggs until fluffy with the sugar and add nutmeg and ginger, then stir the whole mixture together. Pour into a buttered casserole or deep 12 x 8” baking dish.

item.title: Bannock
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups flour (730 ml)
  • 3 level Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 Tbsp sugar
  • 1 cup cold water
  • 1 Tbsp (heaping) lard
  • ¾ tsp salt

Instructions

Mix together the flour, baking powder, salt and sugar in large bowl. Add the lard, cut into small pieces. Mix and make a well in the middle. Add water and mix again. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour. Pat and form into a cake. Deep fry in enough lard or oil so the mixture floats OR bake over coals on a stick like hot dogs. Cook about 20 minutes. Tastes great alone or buttered, with molasses, honey or corn syrup.

item.title: Betty Lance’s Award-winning Banic
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 to 3 cups flour
  • Pinch of salt
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp baking powder
  • 2 Tbsp margarine – melted
  • 1-1/2 cups milk (enough milk to moisten – like dumplings)

Instructions

Bake at 350 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes until golden brown.

Use a cast iron frying pan or an 8 x 8 cake pan.

item.title: Blueberry Soup – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups blueberries
  • 1 cup water
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 3 Tbsp lemon juice
  • Pinch of cinnamon
  • Pinch of cloves
  • ½ tsp coriander
  • 1 cup yogurt or cream

Instructions

In a blender, blend blueberries until pureed. Place in cooking pot and add remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer 5 minutes (covered). Remove from heat and cool. Once cooled, add one cup of yogurt or one cup of cream.

item.title: Bran Bannock
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 cup bran
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp soda
  • ½ tsp cream of tartar
  • 1 Tbsp melted shortening
  • 1 cup sour milk

Instructions

Mix dry ingredients, add the melted shortening, then sour milk. Pour into a greased baking pan and bake in a moderate oven for about 40 minutes.

item.title: Cauliflower Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp crushed garlic
  • 1 cup chopped onions
  • 1 medium cauliflower (separated into florets)
  • 4 cups chicken stock (broth)
  • 2 small potatoes, peeled and chopped
  • ¼ cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 2 Tbsp chopped fresh chives
  • In large non-stick saucepan, heat oil, brown the garlic and onions until softened (approx. 5 minutes). Add cauliflower, stock and potatoes. Bring to a boil, cover and reduce heat and simmer for 25 minutes or until tender.
  • Transfer to food processor and blend until creamy and smooth. Return to saucepan and thin with more stock of desired.
  • Serve with a sprinkle of cheese and chives.

item.title: Cranberry Chutney – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 16-oz package cranberries
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 cup orange juice
  • 1 cup raisins
  • 1 medium chopped granny smith apple
  • 1 Tbsp ground ginger
  • ¼ tsp ground cloves
  • ½ tsp ground nutmeg

Instructions

In a 3-quart saucepan, combine cranberries, water, raisins and shopped apple. Bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer. Cook, covered for 15 minutes or until cranberries pop.

Stir in sugar, orange juice, ginger, nutmeg and cloves. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.

item.title: Creamed Codfish – (from Metis Cookhouse by Gail Lebland_
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cooked codfish, fresh or frozen
  • 3 cups of milk
  • 4 Tbsp of butter or margarine
  • 4 Tbsp of flour

Instructions

Salt and pepper to taste

Melt butter or margarine in top of double boiler, blend in flour, add milk gradually and cook, stirring constantly until sauce thickens.

Add flaked fish. Add salt and pepper.

Serve on toast, or with hot baking powder biscuits or over broiled potatoes.

item.title: Grandma’s Molasses Cookies
item.description:
Instructions

Mix together ½ cup softened butter ½ cup white sugar 1 cup molasses Add 2 tsp baking soda 2 tsp ginger 2-1/2 cups white flour

Mix well then shape i8nto small balls and press down with a fork. Add candy decorations if you wish. Bake in 350 F. Makes 3 dozen cookies.

item.title: Lemon Squares - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup butter or margarine, softened
  • ¼ cup confectioners’ sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • ½ tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 Tbsp lemon juice

Instructions

Heat oven to 350 F. Mix thoroughly flour, butter and confectioners’ sugar. Press in ungreaased square pan, 8x8x2 inches building up ½” edge. Bake 20 minutes.

Beat remaining ingredients about 3 minutes or until light and fluffy. Pour over hot crust.

item.title: Marinade for Venison Steaks – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp dry white wine
  • 5 Tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tsp thyme
  • 1 tsp tarragon
  • 1 tsp chopped parsley
  • Salt & pepper to taste

Instructions

Mix all ingredients of the marinade together. Place steaks in a shallow dish and pour marinade over. Let stand for at least 2 hours, turning steaks once.

Remove steaks from the marinade and season with salt and pepper. Cook on a very hot grill to your liking.

item.title: Mom’s Mock Maple Syrup – Aboriginal Cooking
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 cups water
  • 1 tsp margarine or butter
  • ½ tsp maple extract

Instructions

Combine brown sugar, white sugar and water. Bring to a boil and simmer over medium heat – stirring constantly – for half an hour. Stir in margarine or butter and maple extract. Ready to serve over your pancakes.

item.title: Moose Stew - (metis Cookhouse by Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 lbs moose, cut in stewing pieces
  • 2 carrots
  • ¼ lb butter or margarine
  • 2 parsnips
  • 6 cups water
  • 1 small turnip
  • Salt & Pepper to taste
  • 10 medium potatoes

Instructions

Brown moose meat in hot butter or margarine. Add water, salt & pepper. Simmer 30 minutes then add chopped onions and cook on low for one hour.

But up and add 2 carrots, 2 parsnips, 1 small turnip and 10 medium potatoes and cook for 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.

item.title: New Potatoes with Herbs – (aboriginal Cooking – Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb tiny new potatoes (about 20)
  • 1 Tbs chopped chives
  • 1 Tbs chopped fresh tarragon/basil
  • 1 Tbs lemon juice
  • 2 Tbs olive oil or vegetable oil
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

In saucepan, boil unpeeled potatoes until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain. Add tarragon, chives, lemon juice and oil. Add salt and pepper to taste.

Makes 4 servings.

item.title: Oatmeal Pancakes – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups rolled oats
  • 2 cups 2% milk
  • ½ cup white flour
  • ½ cup whole wheat flour
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp brown sugar
  • 1-1/2 Tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 3 eggs (beaten)
  • ¼ cup melted butter/margarine/Crisco

Instructions

Mix oats and milk. Set aside until milk is absorbed. Flour mixture: combine flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and cinnamon. Mix well. Add eggs and melted butter to oats mixture. Beat well. Add oat mixture to flour mixture, mixing until smooth. Pour batter by ¼ cupfuls onto preheated lightly greased griddle. Bake until fluffy and bubbles break on surface. Pour natural maple syrup over and enjoy. Makes 12 – 15 pancakes.

item.title: Peanut Butter Balls - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup peanut butter
  • 1 cup Rice Krispies
  • 1 Tbsp butter or margarine
  • 1 cup icing sugar
  • ½ cup nuts (optional)
  • 1 cup coconut so roll balls into

Instructions

Cream peanut butter, icing sugar and butter. Add Rice Krispies and nuts. Shape into balls and roll in coconut.

item.title: Peanut Butter Cups (native Cuisine)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup margarine, melted
  • 4 cups icing sugar

Instructions

1-1/2 cups peanut butter 1 cup graham wafer crumbs

Topping: 1 pkg semi-sweet chocolate chips

Mix the above ingredients together. Mixture will be rather dry. Spray cookie sheet with Pam, then press onto the cookie sheet. Melt 1 pkg of semi-sweet chocolate chips and spread on top. Chill for 10 minutes. Remove and cut into squares. Chill for about 2 hours before removing from cookie sheet.

item.title: Pork Cassoulet (from Metis Newspaper)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • Cook Time:- 1 Hr. & 15 Min.
  • Prep. Time:- 10 Min.
  • 2 lbs. Pork loin or sirloin roast, boneless
  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • Salt & Pepper
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 2 garlic cloves, minces
  • 3 cups cranberry juice cocktail
  • 2 – 19 oz/540 ml) cans white kidney beans
  • 2 Tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 Tbsp dry mustard
  • ½ tsp thyme
  • 1 bay leaf
  • fresh parsley, chopped
  • 1 cup fresh or frozen cranberries, chopped (optional)

Instructions

Cut pork into ¾” cubes. Heat oil in large Dutch oven over medium heat. Season Pork cubes with salt and pepper and brown. Transfer to a plate and set aside. Brown onions and garlic until soft, then add cranberry cocktail. Stir to scrape up browned bits; bring to a boil and simmer for 5 – 7 minutes to reduce liquid.

item.title: Pumpkin Bread – Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3-1/2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp cloves
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 2-2/3 cups sugar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • 2 cups canned pumpkin
  • 2.3 cup cooking oil
  • 4 eggs – beaten
  • 2/3 cups water
  • 1 cup walnuts, optional

Instructions

Mix all dry ingredients. Add all wet ingredients and mix well together. Pour into 2 greased 2-pound load pans. Bake approx. 1 hour at 350 degrees F.

item.title: Ruth’s Marinated Cucumbers and Onions – (gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 or 2 Tbsp sugar
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp celery seed
  • 1 large cucumber, thinly slice (about 3 cups)
  • ½ cup thinly sliced onions

Instructions

Combine marinade ingredients. Add cucumber and onions and toss to coat. Cover and chill in fridge for four hours or up to five days. Stir occasionally. Enjoy!

item.title: Ruth’s Tea Biscuits - (gail Leblanc – Metis Cookhouse)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ cup oil or shortening
  • ¾ cup milk

Instructions

Mix flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Cut in the oil; add milk and stir with a fork to make a soft dough.

Gather into a ball and knead several times until smooth. Roll out to ½” thick and cut with a small round cookie cutter ( about 2” diameter).

item.title: Salmon Steak “en Papillote” – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • For oven or BBQ
  • MARINADE:
  • 1 Tbsp brown sugar
  • ½ tsp chopped dill
  • 1 Tbsp Dijon mustard
  • Juice of half a lemon
  • Salt & pepper to taste
  • Half a lemon, thinly sliced
  • 2 Tbsp soft margarine
  • Aluminum foil
  • 1/3 cup dry white or red wine

Instructions

Stir together brown sugar, dill, mustard, lemon juice, salt and pepper Brush over salmon and top each steak with lemon slices Brush margarine on half of two sheets of foil. On each sheet place 2 salmon steaks side by side and baste with wine. Wrap up and seal like a turnover. Cook either in your oven or on the BBQ at 375 F. for approx 15 minutes.

item.title: Shining Tree Corn Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 cup cornmeal
  • 3 Tbsp white sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 beaten egg
  • ¼ cup oil

Instructions

Mix. Put in hot greased frying pan. Lower heat and bake 20 – 25 minutes.

item.title: Sunchokes (jerusalem Artichoke) – (aboriginal Cooking)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • These knotty tubers are the roots of a variety of the sunflower plant and were discovered by Champlain in 1605 in the gardens of the Indians in Cape Cod. They can be served raw or cooked. They are superb in salads and add an interesting touch to soups when thin slices are floated on top.

Instructions

They have a delicate, nutty flavour when served raw. They are an excellent diabetic food since they are high in food value and in the production of levulose sugars, which persons with diabetes can eat.

item.title: Tarragon Roasted Chicken – (aboriginal Cooking –gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 3-lb chicken
  • 2 tsp dried tarragon
  • ¼ cup white wine or apple juice
  • 1 Tbs vegetable oil

Instructions

Cut 1 garlic clove in half. Rub cut sides on outside of chicken. Place garlic in chicken cavity. Sprinkle half of the tarragon in chicken cavity. Tie chicken with string and place in roasting pan.

Mince remaining garlic and combine with remaining tarragon, wine (or apple juice) and oil. Drizzle over chicken and roast in 350 F/180 C oven, basting frequently, for 1-1/4 hours or until juices run clear when chicken is pierced with a fork.

item.title: Three Sisters Soup – (from Take-5 Workshop by Lise Malette)
item.description:
Instructions

Many Aboriginal people know corn, beans & squash as the “Three Sisters”. It is believed that these crops are watched over and kept safe by the spirits of the Three Sisters who are considered to be special gifts from the Creator and should be planted together, eaten together and celebrated together.

1 Tbsp vegetable oil 1 bay leaf l lb beef or wild meat 1 tsp dried basil 1 onion, chopped 6 cups beef stock * 1 cup carrots, diced 1 – 28 oz tin tomatoes 1 cup celery or fennel, chopped 3 cups chopped cabbage 1 cup acorn, butternut or hubbard 1 cup frozen corn squash, cubed 1 – 19 oz tin kidney beans, drained

item.title: Whole Wheat Blueberry Biscuits
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 1 Tbsp granulated sugar
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup soft margarine
  • 1 cup blueberries
  • 1 cup buttermilk or sour milk
  • In bowl, combine all-purpose and whole wheat flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Using fingers, rub in margarine until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
  • Stir in blueberries; add buttermilk and mix lightly. Drop by spoonfuls into 10 mounds. Bake in 425 degree (220 C) oven for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden.
  • Makes 10 biscuits (5 g fat per biscuit)

item.title: Wild Rice & Cheese Casserole – (metis Cooking by Gail Leblanc
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup wild rice
  • 3 cups water
  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • 1 cup grated cheese
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 3 cups sliced mushroom
  • 3 Tbsp butter or margarine
  • 1 – 19 oz tin stewed tomatoes

Instructions

Wash rice under cold water. Put the rice into 3 cups of water and boil for 5 minutes. Let soak in the same water for 1 hour. Cook rice in boiling water for an additional 20 minutes.

item.title: Wild Rice Blueberry Pudding - (tracy Bald)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cooked wild rice
  • 2 cups cream or 2% evaporated milk
  • 6 eggs
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 cup blueberries
  • ¾ cup maple syrup

Instructions

Whisk together the eggs, milk, vanilla and maple syrup. Over medium heat, stir together the wild rice and blueberries in a large sauce pan. Gradually add in milk mixture and stir well. Simmer over medium low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently until pudding thickens. Pour into bowls and cool slightly before serving. Serves 8

item.title: Wild Rice Stuffing for Game Birds – (by Gail Leblanc)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup wild rice
  • 2 chopped onions
  • ¼ cup margarine or butter, melted
  • ½ cup blanched almonds
  • 1 cup sultana raisins
  • 2 tsp poultry seasoing
  • Salt & pepper to taste

Instructions

Wash rice; soak overnight; in the morning drain; cook in boiling salted water until tender. Drain.

Saute onions in hot margarine or butter until golden brown. Add almonds, raisins, poultry seasoning, salt and pepper.

Lightly stuff fowl or game. Cover bird with oiled, heavy brown paper or foil paper, with no water, no cover and no basting, roast in 325 F. oven until done.
